title: Option to have after  ``podman play kube`` wait until containers inside the pod are healthy
body:
### Feature request description

I want to use ``podman play kube`` in our CI, but the application in the container takes some time to start, so I need to wait until the container is healthy. In ``docker compose``, I would use flag ``--wait``. Is there any alternative to this, because I haven't found anything? Or are you planning to implement it?

### Suggest potential solution

_No response_

### Have you considered any alternatives?

We do not want to use any aditional script to check when container is ready.

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body:
Some of this functionality is already supported for Quadlets. If the user sets the `--sdnotify=healthy` then `podman` will wait until the Health check passed before notifying systemd and returning: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/libpod/container_internal.go#L1282. 

We'll need to define a new flag since `--wait` already exists for `kube play` for running the containers in the foreground (maybe `--wait-healthy`)

> Some of this functionality is already supported for Quadlets. If the user sets the `--sdnotify=healthy` then `podman` will wait until the Health check passed before notifying systemd and returning: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/libpod/container_internal.go#L1282.
> 
> We'll need to define a new flag since `--wait` already exists for `kube play` for running the containers in the foreground (maybe `--wait-healthy`)

But ``--sdnotify=healthy`` I am not able to use with ``podman play kube``, right? So it would just leave us with an option to start containers with ``podman`` commands for each one?

> But `--sdnotify=healthy` I am not able to use with `podman play kube`, right? So it would just leave us with an option to start containers with `podman` commands for each one?

Yes, you are right, it is not currently supported. What I meant was that some of the code is already in place in the lower layers and we need to figure our how to expose and use it in `podman kube play`.

### Update. December 2024

I think this actually works by now. Or at least, I have found a solution that (I believe) makes it work. 
Maybe someone can confirm? Or, enlighten me if I've misunderstood something.

I believe the desired feature can be achieved with the setup below but it is not documented. The only reference I found is here: [https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-auto-update.1.html#auto-updates-and-kubernetes-yaml](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-auto-update.1.html#auto-updates-and-kubernetes-yaml)

### Test Setup

- rootless podman setup

**File:** `%h/.config/containers/systemd/bhsh.kube`
```ini
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target

[Unit]
Description=BHSH Test Deployment
After=local-fs.target podman.socket
Wants=podman.socket

[Service]
Restart=always

[Kube]
AutoUpdate=registry
ExitCodePropagation=any
Network=podman
PublishPort=5175:5175
Yaml=%h/.config/containers/systemd/bhsh/deployment.yaml
```

**File:** `%h/.config/containers/systemd/bhsh/deployment.yaml`
```yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: bhsh-deployment-sqlite
  annotations:
    io.containers.autoupdate/bhsh-sqlite: 'registry'
    # 'conmon' works but the service is marked as 'active' (running) immediately, when the pod container is started even though the bhsh-sqlite container is not yet healthy.
    # 'container' did not work. the pod container never starts even though the bhsh-sqlite container is healthy. due to the pod container not starting, the service is never 'active' (running).
    # 'healthy' is not documented but works. the service is marked as 'active' (running) only after the bhsh-sqlite container is healthy.
    io.containers.sdnotify/bhsh-sqlite: 'healthy'
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: bhsh-sqlite
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: bhsh-sqlite
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: bhsh-sqlite
          image: my-image
          livenessProbe:
            exec:
              command:
                - ./healthcheck
                - http://0.0.0.0:5175/health

```

### Test Results

#### with `io.containers.sdnotify/bhsh-sqlite: 'conmon'`

The service immediately reports `active (running)` upon pod startup. BUT the pod does not wait for the bhsh-sqlite container to report as healthy. 

```log

```

#### with `io.containers.sdnotify/bhsh-sqlite: 'container'`

The service never reports `active (running)`. 
The containers are started correctly. 
`podman ps` shows that the bhsh-sqlite container is healthy. 
BUT the pod never starts which explains why the service never reports  `active (running)`. 

```log
Dec 15 12:15:50 podman[608474]: 2024-12-15 12:15:50.737481252 +0000 UTC m=+2.527488392 container init <2ea37...> (image=my-image)
Dec 15 12:15:50 podman[608474]: 2024-12-15 12:15:50.759883378 +0000 UTC m=+2.549890449 container start <2ea37...> (image=my-image)
Dec 15 12:15:50 podman[608474]: 2024-12-15 12:15:50.766948875 +0000 UTC m=+2.556955934 pod start <9bdc3...> (image=, name=bhsh-deployment-sqlite-pod)
Dec 15 12:15:57 bhsh-deployment-sqlite-pod-bhsh-sqlite[608571]: <-- GET /health
Dec 15 12:15:57 bhsh-deployment-sqlite-pod-bhsh-sqlite[608571]: --> GET /health 200 4ms
Dec 15 12:16:01 bhsh-deployment-sqlite-pod-bhsh-sqlite[608571]: <-- GET /health
Dec 15 12:16:01 bhsh-deployment-sqlite-pod-bhsh-sqlite[608571]: --> GET /health 200 1ms
<~75s of successful container healthchecks>
Dec 15 12:17:18 systemd[1352]: bhsh.service: start operation timed out. Terminating.
Dec 15 12:17:18 systemd[1352]: bhsh.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=1/FAILURE
Dec 15 12:17:29 systemd[1352]: bhsh.service: Failed with result 'timeout'.
Dec 15 12:17:29 systemd[1352]: Failed to start BHSH.
```

#### with `io.containers.sdnotify/bhsh-sqlite: 'healthy'`

The pod is only started once the bhsh-sqlite container has reported healthy (i.e. the container healthcheck completes).
The service waits for the pod to start and reports `active (running)` after the pod has started.

```log
Dec 15 12:25:50 systemd[1352]: Starting BHSH...
Dec 15 12:25:50 podman[614154]: 2024-12-15 12:25:50.549853083 +0000 UTC m=+0.086541925 container create <25fa4...> (image=localhost/podman-pause:5.3.1-1733485830, name=0c3bbf373000-service, PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=bhsh.service)

Dec 15 12:25:50 podman[614154]: 2024-12-15 12:25:50.625021306 +0000 UTC m=+0.161710146 pod create <39b31...> (image=my-image, name=bhsh-deployment-sqlite-pod)

Dec 15 12:25:57 bhsh-deployment-sqlite-pod-bhsh-sqlite[614236]: <-- GET /health
Dec 15 12:25:57 bhsh-deployment-sqlite-pod-bhsh-sqlite[614236]: --> GET /health 200 3ms
Dec 15 12:26:03 bhsh-deployment-sqlite-pod-bhsh-sqlite[614236]: <-- GET /health
Dec 15 12:26:03 bhsh-deployment-sqlite-pod-bhsh-sqlite[614236]: --> GET /health 200 1ms
Dec 15 12:26:03 podman[614154]: 2024-12-15 12:26:03.967937493 +0000 UTC m=+13.504626368 pod start <39b31...> (image=my-image, name=bhsh-deployment-sqlite-pod)
Dec 15 12:26:03 systemd[1352]: Started BHSH.
```

The available annotation for [`sd_notify()`](https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/latest/sd_notify.html) support remains underdocumented for `podman play kube` and `.kube` Quadlets.

- https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-kube-play.1.html
- https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.html#kube-units-kube

The `PodmanArgs=--annotation=key=value` gives a little hint at what is possible here.

Notify via `Notify=`/`--sdnotify` is documented for `podman create` and `.container` Quadlets.

- https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-create.1.html#sdnotify-container-conmon-healthy-ignore
- https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.html#notify-defaults-to-false

Both the create command and the Container Quadlet also document the `healthy` value as of now.

To close here, we eventually want to wait until the available annotation `io.containers.sdnotify` is documented for `podman play kube` / `.kube` Quadlets.

- https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/ec0f63c6e59785142d9996bbaa91a4865613f0a6/pkg/domain/infra/abi/play.go#L54
- https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/ec0f63c6e59785142d9996bbaa91a4865613f0a6/pkg/domain/infra/abi/play.go#L110-L111

An example was given in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/19715

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  labels:
    app: test
  name: test_pod
  annotations:
    io.containers.sdnotify: conmon
spec:
  restartPolicy: Never
  containers:
    - name: ctr1
      image: quay.io/libpod/testimage:20221018
      command:
      - true
```
